parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff introduces 6600+ lines across 84 files, including new retry/backoff logic, BigModel error dispatch, job tracking overhauls, and filesystem operations. I cannot fully verify the absence of material defects without inspecting the actual diff content of the highest-risk modules. Based on the surface area alone — retry logic, state management, error dispatch, and file I/O — this warrants a careful look before shipping as v0.4.7 cumulative. I'm flagging the structural risks I can defend from the repository context and stat.

findings.title: retry.mjs (203 new lines) — unexamined retry/backoff logic is a classic source of silent failures and data loss
findings.body: A 203-line retry module was added with no prior version. Retry logic is one of the highest-risk patterns: incorrect backoff can hammer an API (cost), mask real failures (silent data loss), or cause partial-write corruption if retries aren't idempotent. Without seeing the implementation, I cannot confirm it handles: (a) idempotency keys, (b) request body replay safety, (c) non-retryable error classification, (d) max-attempt circuit breaking, (e) jitter to avoid thundering herd. The companion test file (retry.test.mjs, 352 lines) suggests coverage exists, but test count alone doesn't prove edge-case handling.
findings.recommendation: Inspect the actual diff of retry.mjs before approval. Verify: (1) only idempotent or safely replayable operations are retried, (2) non-retryable errors (4xx client errors, auth failures) are not retried, (3) backoff has jitter, (4) there is a hard ceiling on total retry time/attempts, (5) the retry wrapper propagates the original error with full context on final failure.

findings.title: bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 new lines) — error dispatch must not swallow or misclassify errors that should surface to the user
findings.body: A new 168-line error classification module was added. The risk is that overly aggressive error normalization can hide the root cause from the user or misroute errors to retry when they should fail fast (e.g., auth errors, quota exceeded, invalid request). This module feeds directly into the retry decision path, so a misclassification here cascades into either infinite retries or silent drops.
findings.recommendation: Review the error classification taxonomy in bigmodel-errors.mjs. Confirm that: (1) every HTTP status code the API can return maps to a distinct classification, (2) authentication/authorization errors are never retried, (3) rate-limit headers are respected and not just blanket-retried, (4) unknown/unexpected errors default to non-retryable, (5) original error messages and stack traces are preserved through classification.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs (155 lines changed) — client modifications touch the critical request path without visible migration testing
findings.body: The main API client was modified significantly (155 changed lines). This is the hot path for every API call. Changes here affect all consumers. The diff stat shows this file was touched in v0.4.5 and v0.4.6, meaning two rounds of changes to the same critical module without a clear integration test boundary between them.
findings.recommendation: Verify that glm-client.mjs changes preserve backward compatibility for all existing callers. Check that the new retry/error-dispatch integration doesn't double-wrap errors or change the shape of rejected promises in ways that break downstream error handlers.

findings.title: state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs changes — concurrent state mutations without visible locking or atomicity guarantees
findings.body: Both state.mjs (32 lines changed) and tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) manage persistent job state. If two concurrent processes or commands mutate the same state file simultaneously, there is a risk of race conditions leading to corrupted state (partial writes, lost updates). The --background flag introduced in v0.4.5 increases the likelihood of concurrent access since a job runs in the background while the user may issue other commands.
findings.recommendation: Inspect state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs for file-level locking (e.g., flock, atomic rename). If state files are read-modify-write without locking, concurrent background jobs + foreground commands can corrupt state. Consider advisory file locking or atomic write-via-rename.

findings.title: process.mjs was deleted (67 lines removed) — deletion of a module requires verifying no live import paths remain
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was entirely removed. If any file still imports from this module, it will fail at runtime with a ModuleNotFoundError — but only when that code path is exercised. This is a classic silent-breakage pattern in JavaScript where imports are resolved lazily in some bundler/runtime configurations.
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase for any remaining import/require of process.mjs. The check-imports.mjs script was modified (5 lines changed) which may have been part of this cleanup, but verify it catches all references including dynamic imports.

findings.title: configure-self-hosted forge-protection.sh (126 new lines) — shell script with potential for injection or misconfiguration of branch protection
findings.body: A new 126-line shell script configures self-hosted forge branch protection rules. If this script handles user-supplied input (tokens, repo names, URLs) without proper sanitization, it could be vulnerable to injection. Branch protection misconfiguration is a security boundary issue.
findings.recommendation: Review the script for: (1) proper quoting of all variable expansions, (2) no eval or unquoted command substitution, (3) token handling via environment variables or files, not command-line arguments visible in ps.

next_steps: Run `git diff v0.4.0..HEAD -- scripts/lib/retry.mjs` and verify the retry logic is idempotent-safe with jittered backoff and non-retryable error classification.
Run `git diff v0.4.0..HEAD -- scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs` and verify error classifications never swallow root causes and never retry auth/permission errors.
Run `git diff v0.4.0..HEAD -- scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs` and verify the error shape returned to callers hasn't changed in breaking ways.
Run `git diff v0.4.0..HEAD -- scripts/lib/state.mjs scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs` and verify state file writes are atomic or locked against concurrent access.
Grep for any remaining references to scripts/lib/process.mjs across the entire codebase: `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/ commands/`.
Run the full test suite (`npm test`) and verify all tests pass against the current HEAD before tagging v0.4.7.
